message:
There you are admiring your dog in the yard, when suddenly they drop their head and start munching grass like a tiny, four-legged cow. 🐶🌱 Almost every dog owner has watched this and wondered the same thing: "Why on earth are you eating the lawn?"

Here's the reassuring truth: grass-eating is one of the most common — and usually most harmless — things dogs do. It shows up in the majority of dog households, and even wild canids do it, so it has deep evolutionary roots. Most dogs simply seem to like the taste and texture, or are a little bored or curious. You may have heard the old belief that dogs eat grass to make themselves throw up — but vets say dogs don't do it on purpose to vomit. Occasionally a queasy dog will gulp grass quickly and bring it back up, but for most pups, a little grazing comes and goes with no drama at all.

When is it worth a closer look? If the grazing becomes frequent and frantic, comes with repeated vomiting, or your dog just seems off afterward, it's a good idea to check in with your vet to rule out an upset tummy. And of course, steer them away from any lawns that may have been treated with chemicals.

So here's my question: is your dog a casual nibbler or a full-on lawnmower? 🐾💛
